findings.title: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints exposed without auth
findings.description: The /actuator/env and /actuator/heapdump endpoints are publicly accessible on the partner portal, leaking environment variables and enabling heap inspection. Classified Medium in isolation.
findings.evidence: GET /actuator/env -> 200 OK, leaks DB host + spring profiles

findings.title: Vulnerable Spring Framework (Spring4Shell RCE)
findings.description: partner-portal runs a Spring version vulnerable to Spring4Shell. Combined with an exposed actuator and bound request params, this permits remote code execution.

findings.title: Hardcoded database credentials in settlement service
findings.description: A static DB username/password for the settlement DB user is committed in settlement-svc source. The user has elevated DB rights.

findings.title: Settlement DB user has write access to trading ledger
findings.description: The settlement service DB principal holds INSERT/UPDATE grants on the trading-ledger-db, exceeding least-privilege. Allows direct modification of fund/position records.

findings.title: Log4Shell (Log4j2 JNDI RCE) on Trading API host
findings.description: Trading API host runs a vulnerable Log4j2 version. Internet-facing and actively exploited in the wild. Grants remote code execution.

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in KYC document fetcher
findings.description: The KYC service document-preview feature fetches arbitrary URLs server-side, allowing access to internal metadata and the document store endpoint.

findings.title: KYC document store bucket policy allows broad internal read
findings.description: The kyc-document-store S3 bucket grants read to any principal in the VPC rather than only the KYC service role.

findings.title: Apache Struts2 RCE on marketing CMS
findings.description: Public marketing site runs Struts2 vulnerable to CVE-2017-5638. Often dismissed as low-value (public content only).

findings.title: Shared CI deploy token committed in marketing-cms repo
findings.description: A Jenkins deploy token reused across pipelines is committed in the marketing CMS repository, enabling pivot to the build server.

findings.title: Jenkins build server reachable from marketing VPC
findings.description: Network policy allows the (supposedly isolated) marketing VPC to reach the CI/CD build server admin port.

findings.title: Jenkins deploy role can push to production trading API
findings.description: The CI deploy role holds deployment rights to trading-api-prod, so build-server compromise yields production code execution.

findings.title: SQL injection in auth-service login lookup
findings.description: User-supplied identifier is concatenated into a SQL query in the login path, allowing authentication logic bypass and credential table extraction.
findings.evidence: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email='" + email + "'"

findings.title: No rate limiting on auth token endpoint
findings.description: The token issuance endpoint lacks rate limiting, enabling brute force and amplifying the SQLi-based credential extraction.
